verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a single-pass type II transmembrane protein that resides predominantly in the Golgi apparatus where it functions in glycan biosynthesis. However, it has a well-documented cell-surface pool: a membrane-bound form is present on the outer leaflet of various cell types (including tumor cells and sperm), where it acts as a receptor for laminin and other ECM components and mediates cell signaling. The surface form arises from the same TM-anchored protein cycling to the PM. Additionally, a soluble secreted form is shed into serum/milk. The stable TM-anchored surface pool qualifies as dual_localization (dominant Golgi compartment with documented PM minority pool via vesicular cycling).
